channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indian PM Modi's New Zealand Visit and Sports Collaboration with Australia
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi concluded his Australian leg of a three-nation tour with a symbolic visit to the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG), where he and Australian PM Anthony Albanese launche...

item.title: Bangkok bar fire results in 27 fatalities
item.description: A devastating fire broke out at a bar in Bangkok, resulting in the deaths of at least 27 people. The incident, which occurred in a crowded establishment, has raised concerns about safety regulations a...

item.title: Former MP Inky Mark Faces Charges Over Firearms Trafficking and Improper Storage
item.description: Inky Mark, a former federal politician in Manitoba, has been charged with firearms trafficking and related offences following a police search of his home near Dauphin. Authorities seized 439 firearms,...

item.title: KNEC Releases March 2026 Business and Technical Exam Results for Collection
item.description: The Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) has officially released the March 2026 business and technical examination results, instructing institutional heads to collect certificates at New Mitihan...

item.title: School Classes Suspended Again in Tacloban Due to Safety Concerns
item.description: The suspension of classes at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City has been extended due to ongoing safety concerns. This follows a previous incident where a shooting threat prompted the scho...

item.title: Nicola Sturgeon refuses to release police statement amid SNP financial scandal
item.description: Nicola Sturgeon, the former First Minister of Scotland, has refused to release the written statement she provided to police investigating the SNP's financial misconduct. Her lawyer, Aamer Anwar, confi...

item.title: Australian Author's New Novel Draws on Golden Age of Mysteries
item.description: Australian author Benjamin Stevenson's latest novel, 'Everyone In This Bank Is A Thief,' blends classic mystery elements with modern crime fiction. The story revolves around a chaotic bank heist invol...

item.title: Kannada Play 'This Dying Emperor' Explores Mughal Decline and Political Intrigue
item.description: The article presents an excerpt from P Lankesh's Kannada play, translated into English, titled 'This Dying Emperor Possesses Neither Army Nor Throne.' The play delves into the twilight of the Mughal E...

item.title: Warframe and Soulframe Get Major Updates at TennoCon 2026
item.description: Digital Extremes unveiled significant updates for both Warframe and Soulframe during TennoCon 2026, focusing on expansive content and narrative depth. Warframe's Tau expansion introduces a new galaxy ...

item.title: Dangote Refinery Shifts to USD for Petrol Sales, Ending Naira Pricing
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery has officially switched to dollar-denominated pricing for its petrol and diesel products, marking a significant shift in Nigeria's fuel market. The refinery set the ex-depot...

item.title: Angat Dam Water Level Slightly Increases, Below Critical Threshold
item.description: The water level of Angat Dam rose slightly to 156.21 meters on July 14, 2026, according to records from the provincial disaster risk reduction and management office (PDRRMO). This marks a marginal inc...

item.title: UK to amend deportation law for Rochdale grooming gang ringleader
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has announced plans to reform UK immigration laws to enable the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the former ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang. Ahmed, who was jailed for...

item.title: Scientists Detect Sugar in Milky Way, Shedding Light on Life's Origins
item.description: A team of astrochemists has identified naturally occurring sugar, specifically erythrulose, in the interstellar medium near the center of the Milky Way. This discovery, published in *Nature Astronomy*...

item.title: List of Hollywood Actors Known for Being Difficult to Work With
item.description: The article lists 15 Hollywood actors renowned for their challenging behavior on set. Chevy Chase is noted for his fiery confrontations, while Edward Norton's clashes with directors over creative cont...

item.title: Spanish PM highlights need for wildfire prevention strategies
item.description: The Spanish Prime Minister has called for enhanced prevention measures to mitigate the risk of deadly wildfires, following a recent incident that claimed lives. The statement emphasizes the importance...

item.title: PIC Suspends CEO and CIO Following Governance Crisis
item.description: The Public Investment Corporation (PIC), South Africa's largest asset manager overseeing R3.6 trillion in government pension and social funds, has placed its chief executive Patrick Dlamini and chief ...

item.title: Sen. Rick Scott Criticizes Senate Workweek and Recesses
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott (R-Florida) has publicly criticized the U.S. Senate for its short workweek and excessive recesses, arguing that the chamber's operations are inefficient and detrimental to legislative ...

item.title: Zanzibar's President Urges Tanzanian Firms to Expand Global Trade
item.description: Zanzibar’s President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi has called on Tanzanian businesses to expand their operations beyond domestic borders to tap into international trade opportunities. Speaking at the 50th Dar...

item.title: Freshfields Restructures Pay, Cuts Partnerships Amid Growth Efforts
item.description: UK law firm Freshfields has implemented significant changes to its partnership structure following a major overhaul of its compensation system. The restructuring, which introduced a performance-based ...

item.title: Trump Ends Press Conference After Confrontation with CNN Anchor Over Iran Strikes
item.description: President Donald Trump abruptly ended a White House press conference on Monday after CNN anchor Kaitlan Collins pressed him about U.S. military actions in Iran. During the Oval Office briefing, Collin...
